metadata.description: Cross-city avian vocal adaptation dataset comparing vertical (Hangzhou) vs horizontal (Washington DC) urban acoustic environments.

recordings.reverb_profile: High vertical reflection from 30+ story residential towers; narrow street canyon amplifies mid-frequency reverb.
recordings.notes: Recorded during dawn chorus. Clear temporal extension in syllable spacing compared to rural baseline.

recordings.reverb_profile: Moderate vertical reflection between commercial towers; wider street reduces canyon effect but maintains reverb.
recordings.notes: Slightly less reverb than HZ-001 due to wider street. Temporal adaptation still evident but less pronounced.

recordings.location_cn: 岩溪公园 · 城市边缘
recordings.reverb_profile: Minimal vertical reflection; dominant horizontal noise from Connecticut Ave traffic corridor.
recordings.notes: Clear spectral upshift compared to rural populations. Traffic noise dominant below 2 kHz masks lower-frequency vocalizations.

recordings.location_cn: 乔治城水滨 · 运河步道
recordings.reverb_profile: Open waterfront with strong horizontal noise propagation from M Street traffic; water surface reflection adds broadband masking.
recordings.notes: Highest frequency shift in dataset. Consistent with maximum horizontal noise exposure at urban waterfront.
